title: Asthma action plan documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or diagnosed asthma, excluding severe asthma and acute attacks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist diagnosis, monitoring, and chronic asthma management.
source.exclusions: Exact for chronic asthma assessment and monitoring; excludes severe asthma and acute attacks and is not directly validated for UAE pathways.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports inhaler-technique and adherence review at every review and clinician-documented personalised action-plan and trigger discussion without mapping medicine names or doses.

assessment.text: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.
assessment.rationale: Supports asthma-control monitoring for asthma action-plan documentation; no decision is generated.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.6.4 — monitoring asthma control and uncontrolled asthma

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

assessment.text: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.
assessment.rationale: Supports uncontrolled-asthma review and referral context for asthma action-plan documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.
